title: Critical Bugs Batch Fix — Batch 2 Completed
objective: Clear all 20 remaining critical bugs from BUG_REPORT.md in 3 batches
completed: Reviewed BUG_REPORT.md — identified 20 remaining critical bugs (top 10 already fixed in prior session)
Created implementation_plan.md with 3-batch strategy (12 easy + 5 medium + 3 high-risk)
Analyzed real-world user impact of each bug group (ANR, data loss, UI crash, resource leak, performance)
Added serena MCP server config to C:\Users\ASUS\.cursor\mcp.json
Added Prompt Optimizer & Compressor + Ponytail Ruleset to .agents/AGENTS.md (prior session)

Batch 1 (12 bugs): Fixed cursor leaks in LocalVehicleDbHelper, transaction atomicity in MemoryDbHelper/MeetingDbHelper, optimized updateMemoryPin direct SQL path, offloaded MemoryViewModel IO, serviceScope.launch for MeetingRecordingService runBlocking, released AudioPlayer track on cancel, added AttentionState idle timeouts, SituationLogManager log rotation, and implemented Closeable on PerceptionEngine

Batch 2 (5 bugs): Hardened GeminiToolDispatcher by wrapping save/query/time handlers in try-catch blocks to prevent crashes; introduced HttpURLConnection readTextWithTimeout wrapper setting 10s connect/read timeouts to prevent network hang ANR; added Notification-based fallback for API 29+ background activity launch of open_camera; offloaded MainActivity image processing (rotate, scale, compress, base64) to background dispatcher using lifecycleScope.launch(Dispatchers.Default); resolved permission request code conflation by assigning distinct request codes (EXTRA_PERMISSIONS_REQUEST_CODE) to call/location permissions
Wrote and ran 16 unit/static-analysis tests in DatabaseCodeTest, all passing along with existing 28 tests (total 44 passing)
pending: Batch 3 (3 bugs): AppPreferences AES-ECB→AES-GCM + ANDROID_ID key migration, DatingAnalysisOrchestrator parallel execution
Run full test suite after Batch 3

artifacts.description: Master bug list — 30 critical, 35+ major, 24 minor issues
Workspace rules: TDD, Prompt Optimizer, Ponytail Ruleset

decisions.decision: Fix bugs in 3 batches: easy wins → medium → high-risk
decisions.rationale: Maximizes value delivery early; defers risky encryption migration to last batch

decisions.decision: AES-ECB → AES-GCM migration will use try-new-decrypt → fallback-old-decrypt → re-encrypt strategy
decisions.rationale: Prevents permanent data loss for existing users while upgrading security

knowledge: Top 10 bugs already fixed in prior session (commits 216fd41, 153a899)
Also fixed in prior session: WebSocket handshake leak, callback threading, ConcurrentHashMap, Kotlin serialization plugin
All 44 unit/static-analysis tests pass as of this session
LocalVehicleDbHelper is in utils/ package, not root package
MemoryManager.updateMemoryPin() loads ALL memories via getMemoryList() to toggle 1 row
AudioPlayer already has releaseAudioTrack() and abandonAudioFocus() methods — just not called in finally block
MeetingRecordingService.stopDeepgramStreaming() uses runBlocking on main thread at line 449
MemoryViewModel.loadData() calls SQLite synchronously in init{} block

assumptions.assumption: User will approve implementation plan before execution begins
AES-ECB → AES-GCM migration with fallback is acceptable approach

risks.risk: AES encryption migration may fail silently if old data is corrupted
risks.mitigation: Log migration failures; keep old data as backup before re-encrypting

risks.risk: GeminiToolDispatcher background activity change alters user camera flow
risks.mitigation: Use PendingIntent via notification — minimal UX change

open_questions: Should we implement AES-GCM migration with automatic fallback, or prompt user to re-enter keys?

agents.name: Antigravity (Gemini 3.5 Flash)
agents.responsibility: Plan and execute critical bug fixes in 3 batches

memory_snapshot: User prefers Thai communication with internal English compression
Ponytail Ruleset enforced: minimal code, Decision Ladder, no over-engineering
TDD required: write tests first, then fix code
context_summary: Successfully completed Batch 2 fixes (5 medium complexity bugs related to tool exceptions, connection/read timeouts, API 29 background starts, main-thread image processing, and permission result conflation). All 44 tests pass. Ready to proceed to Batch 3.
